text.fullText:
To Whom It May Concern,

As the owner of Trans Sohal Logistics, a trucking company with a dedicated fleet and over 20 years of experience in the transportation industry, I am writing to express my strong support for the proposed rule under Docket No. FMCSA-2023-0257 (RIN 2126-AC63) regarding transparency in property broker transactions.

Transparency is essential for maintaining fairness, trust, and sustainability within the logistics industry. For far too long, carriers have operated without access to critical transaction details, creating an imbalance of power that disproportionately benefits brokers while leaving carriers at a disadvantage. This rule is a necessary step toward restoring equity and ensuring that carriers are compensated fairly for the vital role they play in keeping goods moving across the nation.

Without visibility into broker margins, carriers are unable to assess whether they are receiving a fair share of the freight charges. This lack of transparency not only undermines carriers' negotiating power but also threatens the viability of small and independent trucking businesses—the backbone of the American supply chain.

Honest brokers should welcome this rule. Those operating with integrity have nothing to lose from transparency and everything to gain in fostering trust with their carrier partners. This rule will not punish ethical business practices but will instead shine a light on unfair dealings that have persisted in the shadows for too long.

We appreciate the FMCSA’s leadership in addressing this critical issue and urge the agency to move forward with the implementation of this rule without delay. By standing with carriers, the FMCSA can help create a more balanced, competitive, and sustainable freight industry for all stakeholders.

Thank you for your attention to this matter and for considering the perspectives of those on the front lines of transportation.

Raj Sohal
Owner & Operator
Trans Sohal Logistics
